1.text: How doth the city<S>5892</S> sit<S>3427</S> solitary<S>910</S>, that was full<S>7227</S> of people<S>5971</S>! how is she become as a widow<S>490</S>! she that was great<S>7227</S> among the nations<S>1471</S>, and princess<S>8282</S> among the provinces<S>4082</S>, how is she become tributary<S>4522</S>!

She weepeth<S>1058</S> sore<S>1058</S> in the night<S>3915</S>, and her tears<S>1832</S> are on her cheeks<S>3895</S>: among all her lovers<S>157</S> she hath none to comfort<S>5162</S> her: all her friends<S>7453</S> have dealt treacherously<S>898</S> with her, they are become her enemies<S>341</S>.

Judah<S>3063</S> is gone into captivity<S>1540</S> because of affliction<S>6040</S>, and because of great<S>7230</S> servitude<S>5656</S>: she dwelleth<S>3427</S> among the heathen<S>1471</S>, she findeth<S>4672</S> no rest<S>4494</S>: all her persecutors<S>7291</S> overtook<S>5381</S> her between the straits<S>4712</S>. <sup>because of great: Heb. for the greatness of servitude</sup>

The ways<S>1870</S> of Zion<S>6726</S> do mourn<S>57</S>, because none come<S>935</S> to the solemn feasts<S>4150</S>: all her gates<S>8179</S> are desolate<S>8074</S>: her priests<S>3548</S> sigh<S>584</S>, her virgins<S>1330</S> are afflicted<S>3013</S>, and she is in bitterness<S>4843</S>.

Her adversaries<S>6862</S> are the chief<S>7218</S>, her enemies<S>341</S> prosper<S>7951</S>; for the LORD<S>3068</S> hath afflicted<S>3013</S> her for the multitude<S>7230</S> of her transgressions<S>6588</S>: her children<S>5768</S> are gone<S>1980</S> into captivity<S>7628</S> before<S>6440</S> the enemy<S>6862</S>.

And from the daughter<S>1323</S> of Zion<S>6726</S> all her beauty<S>1926</S> is departed<S>3318</S>: her princes<S>8269</S> are become like harts<S>354</S> that find<S>4672</S> no pasture<S>4829</S>, and they are gone<S>3212</S> without strength<S>3581</S> before<S>6440</S> the pursuer<S>7291</S>.

Jerusalem<S>3389</S> remembered<S>2142</S> in the days<S>3117</S> of her affliction<S>6040</S> and of her miseries<S>4788</S> all her pleasant things<S>4262</S> that she had in the days<S>3117</S> of old<S>6924</S>, when her people<S>5971</S> fell<S>5307</S> into the hand<S>3027</S> of the enemy<S>6862</S>, and none did help<S>5826</S> her: the adversaries<S>6862</S> saw<S>7200</S> her, and did mock<S>7832</S> at her sabbaths<S>4868</S>. <sup>pleasant: or, desirable</sup>

Jerusalem<S>3389</S> hath grievously<S>2399</S> sinned<S>2398</S>; therefore she is removed<S>5206</S>: all that honoured<S>3513</S> her despise<S>2107</S> her, because they have seen<S>7200</S> her nakedness<S>6172</S>: yea, she sigheth<S>584</S>, and turneth<S>7725</S> backward<S>268</S>. <sup>is: Heb. is become a removing, or, wandering</sup>

Her filthiness<S>2932</S> is in her skirts<S>7757</S>; she remembereth<S>2142</S> not her last end<S>319</S>; therefore she came down<S>3381</S> wonderfully<S>6382</S>: she had no comforter<S>5162</S>. O LORD<S>3068</S>, behold<S>7200</S> my affliction<S>6040</S>: for the enemy<S>341</S> hath magnified<S>1431</S> himself.

The adversary<S>6862</S> hath spread out<S>6566</S> his hand<S>3027</S> upon all her pleasant things<S>4261</S>: for she hath seen<S>7200</S> that the heathen<S>1471</S> entered<S>935</S> into her sanctuary<S>4720</S>, whom thou didst command<S>6680</S> that they should not enter<S>935</S> into thy congregation<S>6951</S>. <sup>pleasant: or, desirable</sup>

All her people<S>5971</S> sigh<S>584</S>, they seek<S>1245</S> bread<S>3899</S>; they have given<S>5414</S> their pleasant things<S>4261</S> <S>4262</S> for meat<S>400</S> to relieve<S>7725</S> the soul<S>5315</S>: see<S>7200</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, and consider<S>5027</S>; for I am become vile<S>2151</S>. <sup>to: or, to make the soul to come again</sup>

Is it nothing to you, all ye that pass<S>5674</S> by<S>1870</S>? behold<S>5027</S>, and see<S>7200</S> if there be<S>3426</S> any sorrow<S>4341</S> like unto my sorrow<S>4341</S>, which is done<S>5953</S> unto me, wherewith the LORD<S>3068</S> hath afflicted<S>3013</S> me in the day<S>3117</S> of his fierce<S>2740</S> anger<S>639</S>. <sup>Is it: or, It is nothing</sup> <sup>pass by: Heb. pass by the way?</sup>

From above<S>4791</S> hath he sent<S>7971</S> fire<S>784</S> into my bones<S>6106</S>, and it prevaileth<S>7287</S> against them: he hath spread<S>6566</S> a net<S>7568</S> for my feet<S>7272</S>, he hath turned<S>7725</S> me back<S>268</S>: he hath made<S>5414</S> me desolate<S>8074</S> and faint<S>1739</S> all the day<S>3117</S>.

The yoke<S>5923</S> of my transgressions<S>6588</S> is bound<S>8244</S> by his hand<S>3027</S>: they are wreathed<S>8276</S>, and come up<S>5927</S> upon my neck<S>6677</S>: he hath made my strength<S>3581</S> to fall<S>3782</S>, the Lord<S>136</S> hath delivered<S>5414</S> me into their hands<S>3027</S>, from whom I am not able<S>3201</S> to rise up<S>6965</S>.

The Lord<S>136</S> hath trodden under foot<S>5541</S> all my mighty<S>47</S> men in the midst<S>7130</S> of me: he hath called<S>7121</S> an assembly<S>4150</S> against me to crush<S>7665</S> my young men<S>970</S>: the Lord<S>136</S> hath trodden<S>1869</S> the virgin<S>1330</S>, the daughter<S>1323</S> of Judah<S>3063</S>, as in a winepress<S>1660</S>. <sup>the virgin: or, the winepress of the virgin, etc</sup>

For these things I weep<S>1058</S>; mine eye<S>5869</S>, mine eye<S>5869</S> runneth down<S>3381</S> with water<S>4325</S>, because the comforter<S>5162</S> that should relieve<S>7725</S> my soul<S>5315</S> is far<S>7368</S> from me: my children<S>1121</S> are desolate<S>8074</S>, because the enemy<S>341</S> prevailed<S>1396</S>. <sup>relieve: Heb. bring back</sup>

Zion<S>6726</S> spreadeth forth<S>6566</S> her hands<S>3027</S>, and there is none to comfort<S>5162</S> her: the LORD<S>3068</S> hath commanded<S>6680</S> concerning Jacob<S>3290</S>, that his adversaries<S>6862</S> should be round about<S>5439</S> him: Jerusalem<S>3389</S> is as a menstruous woman<S>5079</S> among them.

The LORD<S>3068</S> is righteous<S>6662</S>; for I have rebelled<S>4784</S> against his commandment<S>6310</S>: hear<S>8085</S>, I pray you, all people<S>5971</S>, and behold<S>7200</S> my sorrow<S>4341</S>: my virgins<S>1330</S> and my young men<S>970</S> are gone<S>1980</S> into captivity<S>7628</S>. <sup>commandment: Heb. mouth</sup>

I called<S>7121</S> for my lovers<S>157</S>, but they deceived<S>7411</S> me: my priests<S>3548</S> and mine elders<S>2205</S> gave up the ghost<S>1478</S> in the city<S>5892</S>, while they sought<S>1245</S> their meat<S>400</S> to relieve<S>7725</S> their souls<S>5315</S>.

Behold<S>7200</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>; for I am in distress<S>6887</S>: my bowels<S>4578</S> are troubled<S>2560</S>; mine heart<S>3820</S> is turned<S>2015</S> within<S>7130</S> me; for I have grievously<S>4784</S> rebelled<S>4784</S>: abroad<S>2351</S> the sword<S>2719</S> bereaveth<S>7921</S>, at home<S>1004</S> there is as death<S>4194</S>.

They have heard<S>8085</S> that I sigh<S>584</S>: there is none to comfort<S>5162</S> me: all mine enemies<S>341</S> have heard<S>8085</S> of my trouble<S>7451</S>; they are glad<S>7797</S> that thou hast done<S>6213</S> it: thou wilt bring<S>935</S> the day<S>3117</S> that thou hast called<S>7121</S>, and they shall be like<S>3644</S> unto me. <sup>called: or, proclaimed</sup>

Let all their wickedness<S>7451</S> come<S>935</S> before<S>6440</S> thee; and do<S>5953</S> unto them, as thou hast done<S>5953</S> unto me for all my transgressions<S>6588</S>: for my sighs<S>585</S> are many<S>7227</S>, and my heart<S>3820</S> is faint<S>1742</S>.

2.text: How hath the Lord<S>136</S> covered<S>5743</S> the daughter<S>1323</S> of Zion<S>6726</S> with a cloud<S>5743</S> in his anger<S>639</S>, and cast down<S>7993</S> from heaven<S>8064</S> unto the earth<S>776</S> the beauty<S>8597</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, and remembered<S>2142</S> not his footstool<S>1916</S> <S>7272</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of his anger<S>639</S>!

The Lord<S>136</S> hath swallowed up<S>1104</S> all the habitations<S>4999</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>, and hath not pitied<S>2550</S>: he hath thrown down<S>2040</S> in his wrath<S>5678</S> the strong holds<S>4013</S> of the daughter<S>1323</S> of Judah<S>3063</S>; he hath brought them down<S>5060</S> to the ground<S>776</S>: he hath polluted<S>2490</S> the kingdom<S>4467</S> and the princes<S>8269</S> thereof. <sup>brought: Heb. made to touch</sup>

He hath cut off<S>1438</S> in his fierce<S>2750</S> anger<S>639</S> all the horn<S>7161</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>: he hath drawn<S>7725</S> back<S>268</S> his right hand<S>3225</S> from before<S>6440</S> the enemy<S>341</S>, and he burned<S>1197</S> against Jacob<S>3290</S> like a flaming<S>3852</S> fire<S>784</S>, which devoureth<S>398</S> round about<S>5439</S>.

He hath bent<S>1869</S> his bow<S>7198</S> like an enemy<S>341</S>: he stood<S>5324</S> with his right hand<S>3225</S> as an adversary<S>6862</S>, and slew<S>2026</S> all that were pleasant<S>4261</S> to the eye<S>5869</S> in the tabernacle<S>168</S> of the daughter<S>1323</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>: he poured out<S>8210</S> his fury<S>2534</S> like fire<S>784</S>. <sup>all: Heb. all the desirable of the eye</sup>

The Lord<S>136</S> was as an enemy<S>341</S>: he hath swallowed up<S>1104</S> Israel<S>3478</S>, he hath swallowed up<S>1104</S> all her palaces<S>759</S>: he hath destroyed<S>7843</S> his strong holds<S>4013</S>, and hath increased<S>7235</S> in the daughter<S>1323</S> of Judah<S>3063</S> mourning<S>8386</S> and lamentation<S>592</S>.

And he hath violently taken away<S>2554</S> his tabernacle<S>7900</S>, as if it were of a garden<S>1588</S>: he hath destroyed<S>7843</S> his places of the assembly<S>4150</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> hath caused the solemn feasts<S>4150</S> and sabbaths<S>7676</S> to be forgotten<S>7911</S> in Zion<S>6726</S>, and hath despised<S>5006</S> in the indignation<S>2195</S> of his anger<S>639</S> the king<S>4428</S> and the priest<S>3548</S>. <sup>tabernacle: or, hedge</sup>

The Lord<S>136</S> hath cast off<S>2186</S> his altar<S>4196</S>, he hath abhorred<S>5010</S> his sanctuary<S>4720</S>, he hath given up<S>5462</S> into the hand<S>3027</S> of the enemy<S>341</S> the walls<S>2346</S> of her palaces<S>759</S>; they have made<S>5414</S> a noise<S>6963</S> in the house<S>1004</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>, as in the day<S>3117</S> of a solemn feast<S>4150</S>. <sup>given up: Heb. shut up</sup>

The LORD<S>3068</S> hath purposed<S>2803</S> to destroy<S>7843</S> the wall<S>2346</S> of the daughter<S>1323</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>: he hath stretched out<S>5186</S> a line<S>6957</S>, he hath not withdrawn<S>7725</S> his hand<S>3027</S> from destroying<S>1104</S>: therefore he made the rampart<S>2426</S> and the wall<S>2346</S> to lament<S>56</S>; they languished<S>535</S> together<S>3162</S>. <sup>destroying: Heb. swallowing up</sup>

Her gates<S>8179</S> are sunk<S>2883</S> into the ground<S>776</S>; he hath destroyed<S>6</S> and broken<S>7665</S> her bars<S>1280</S>: her king<S>4428</S> and her princes<S>8269</S> are among the Gentiles<S>1471</S>: the law<S>8451</S> is no more; her prophets<S>5030</S> also find<S>4672</S> no vision<S>2377</S> from the LORD<S>3068</S>.

The elders<S>2205</S> of the daughter<S>1323</S> of Zion<S>6726</S> sit<S>3427</S> upon the ground<S>776</S>, and keep silence<S>1826</S>: they have cast up<S>5927</S> dust<S>6083</S> upon their heads<S>7218</S>; they have girded<S>2296</S> themselves with sackcloth<S>8242</S>: the virgins<S>1330</S> of Jerusalem<S>3389</S> hang down<S>3381</S> their heads<S>7218</S> to the ground<S>776</S>.

Mine eyes<S>5869</S> do fail<S>3615</S> with tears<S>1832</S>, my bowels<S>4578</S> are troubled<S>2560</S>, my liver<S>3516</S> is poured<S>8210</S> upon the earth<S>776</S>, for the destruction<S>7667</S> of the daughter<S>1323</S> of my people<S>5971</S>; because the children<S>5768</S> and the sucklings<S>3243</S> swoon<S>5848</S> in the streets<S>7339</S> of the city<S>7151</S>. <sup>swoon: or, faint</sup>

They say<S>559</S> to their mothers<S>517</S>, Where is corn<S>1715</S> and wine<S>3196</S>? when they swooned<S>5848</S> as the wounded<S>2491</S> in the streets<S>7339</S> of the city<S>5892</S>, when their soul<S>5315</S> was poured out<S>8210</S> into their mothers'<S>517</S> bosom<S>2436</S>.

What thing shall I take to witness<S>5749</S> <S>5749</S> for thee? what thing<S>4100</S> shall I liken<S>1819</S> to thee, O daughter<S>1323</S> of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>? what shall I equal<S>7737</S> to thee, that I may comfort<S>5162</S> thee, O virgin<S>1330</S> daughter<S>1323</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>? for thy breach<S>7667</S> is great<S>1419</S> like the sea<S>3220</S>: who can heal<S>7495</S> thee?

Thy prophets<S>5030</S> have seen<S>2372</S> vain<S>7723</S> and foolish things<S>8602</S> for thee: and they have not discovered<S>1540</S> thine iniquity<S>5771</S>, to turn away<S>7725</S> thy captivity<S>7622</S> <S>7622</S>; but have seen<S>2372</S> for thee false<S>7723</S> burdens<S>4864</S> and causes of banishment<S>4065</S>.

All that pass<S>5674</S> by clap<S>5606</S> their hands<S>3709</S> at thee<S>1870</S>; they hiss<S>8319</S> and wag<S>5128</S> their head<S>7218</S> at the daughter<S>1323</S> of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>, saying, Is this the city<S>5892</S> that men call<S>559</S> The perfection<S>3632</S> of beauty<S>3308</S>, The joy<S>4885</S> of the whole earth<S>776</S>? <sup>by: Heb. by the way</sup>

All thine enemies<S>341</S> have opened<S>6475</S> their mouth<S>6310</S> against thee: they hiss<S>8319</S> and gnash<S>2786</S> the teeth<S>8127</S>: they say<S>559</S>, We have swallowed her up<S>1104</S>: certainly<S>389</S> this is the day<S>3117</S> that we looked for<S>6960</S>; we have found<S>4672</S>, we have seen<S>7200</S> it.

The LORD<S>3068</S> hath done<S>6213</S> that which he had devised<S>2161</S>; he hath fulfilled<S>1214</S> his word<S>565</S> that he had commanded<S>6680</S> in the days<S>3117</S> of old<S>6924</S>: he hath thrown down<S>2040</S>, and hath not pitied<S>2550</S>: and he hath caused thine enemy<S>341</S> to rejoice<S>8055</S> over thee, he hath set up<S>7311</S> the horn<S>7161</S> of thine adversaries<S>6862</S>.

Their heart<S>3820</S> cried<S>6817</S> unto the Lord<S>136</S>, O wall<S>2346</S> of the daughter<S>1323</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>, let tears<S>1832</S> run down<S>3381</S> like a river<S>5158</S> day<S>3119</S> and night<S>3915</S>: give<S>5414</S> thyself no rest<S>6314</S>; let not the apple<S>1323</S> of thine eye<S>5869</S> cease<S>1826</S>.

Arise<S>6965</S>, cry out<S>7442</S> in the night<S>3915</S>: in the beginning<S>7218</S> of the watches<S>821</S> pour out<S>8210</S> thine heart<S>3820</S> like water<S>4325</S> before<S>5227</S> the face<S>6440</S> of the Lord<S>136</S>: lift up<S>5375</S> thy hands<S>3709</S> toward him for the life<S>5315</S> of thy young children<S>5768</S>, that faint<S>5848</S> for hunger<S>7458</S> in the top<S>7218</S> of every street<S>2351</S>.

Behold<S>7200</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, and consider<S>5027</S> to whom thou hast done<S>5953</S> this<S>3541</S>. Shall the women<S>802</S> eat<S>398</S> their fruit<S>6529</S>, and children<S>5768</S> of a span long<S>2949</S>? shall the priest<S>3548</S> and the prophet<S>5030</S> be slain<S>2026</S> in the sanctuary<S>4720</S> of the Lord<S>136</S>? <sup>of a span: or, swaddled with their hands?</sup>

The young<S>5288</S> and the old<S>2205</S> lie<S>7901</S> on the ground<S>776</S> in the streets<S>2351</S>: my virgins<S>1330</S> and my young men<S>970</S> are fallen<S>5307</S> by the sword<S>2719</S>; thou hast slain<S>2026</S> them in the day<S>3117</S> of thine anger<S>639</S>; thou hast killed<S>2873</S>, and not pitied<S>2550</S>.

Thou hast called<S>7121</S> as in a solemn<S>4150</S> day<S>3117</S> my terrors<S>4032</S> round about<S>5439</S>, so that in the day<S>3117</S> of the LORD'S<S>3068</S> anger<S>639</S> none escaped<S>6412</S> nor remained<S>8300</S>: those that I have swaddled<S>2946</S> and brought up<S>7235</S> hath mine enemy<S>341</S> consumed<S>3615</S>.

3.text: I am the man<S>1397</S> that hath seen<S>7200</S> affliction<S>6040</S> by the rod<S>7626</S> of his wrath<S>5678</S>.
He hath led<S>5090</S> me, and brought<S>3212</S> me into darkness<S>2822</S>, but not into light<S>216</S>.
Surely against me is he turned<S>7725</S>; he turneth<S>2015</S> his hand<S>3027</S> against me all the day<S>3117</S>.
My flesh<S>1320</S> and my skin<S>5785</S> hath he made old<S>1086</S>; he hath broken<S>7665</S> my bones<S>6106</S>.
He hath builded against<S>1129</S> me, and compassed<S>5362</S> me with gall<S>7219</S> and travail<S>8513</S>.
He hath set<S>3427</S> me in dark places<S>4285</S>, as they that be dead<S>4191</S> of old<S>5769</S>.
He hath hedged<S>1443</S> me about, that I cannot get out<S>3318</S>: he hath made my chain<S>5178</S> heavy<S>3513</S>.
Also when I cry<S>2199</S> and shout<S>7768</S>, he shutteth out<S>5640</S> my prayer<S>8605</S>.
He hath inclosed<S>1443</S> my ways<S>1870</S> with hewn stone<S>1496</S>, he hath made my paths<S>5410</S> crooked<S>5753</S>.
He was unto me as a bear<S>1677</S> lying in wait<S>693</S>, and as a lion<S>738</S> in secret places<S>4565</S>.
He hath turned aside<S>5493</S> my ways<S>1870</S>, and pulled me in pieces<S>6582</S>: he hath made<S>7760</S> me desolate<S>8074</S>.
He hath bent<S>1869</S> his bow<S>7198</S>, and set<S>5324</S> me as a mark<S>4307</S> for the arrow<S>2671</S>.
He hath caused the arrows<S>1121</S> of his quiver<S>827</S> to enter<S>935</S> into my reins<S>3629</S>. <sup>arrows: Heb. sons</sup>
I was a derision<S>7814</S> to all my people<S>5971</S>; and their song<S>5058</S> all the day<S>3117</S>.
He hath filled<S>7646</S> me with bitterness<S>4844</S>, he hath made me drunken<S>7301</S> with wormwood<S>3939</S>. <sup>bitterness: Heb. bitternesses</sup>
He hath also broken<S>1638</S> my teeth<S>8127</S> with gravel stones<S>2687</S>, he hath covered<S>3728</S> me with ashes<S>665</S>. <sup>covered: or, rolled me in the ashes</sup>
And thou hast removed<S>2186</S> my soul<S>5315</S> far off<S>2186</S> from peace<S>7965</S>: I forgat<S>5382</S> prosperity<S>2896</S>. <sup>prosperity: Heb. good</sup>
And I said<S>559</S>, My strength<S>5331</S> and my hope<S>8431</S> is perished<S>6</S> from the LORD<S>3068</S>:
Remembering<S>2142</S> mine affliction<S>6040</S> and my misery<S>4788</S>, the wormwood<S>3939</S> and the gall<S>7219</S>. <sup>Remembering: or, Remember</sup>
My soul<S>5315</S> hath them still<S>2142</S> in remembrance<S>2142</S>, and is humbled<S>7743</S> in me. <sup>humbled: Heb. bowed</sup>
This I recall<S>7725</S> to my mind<S>3820</S>, therefore have I hope<S>3176</S>. <sup>recall: Heb. make to return to my heart</sup>
It is of the LORD'S<S>3068</S> mercies<S>2617</S> that we are not consumed<S>8552</S>, because his compassions<S>7356</S> fail<S>3615</S> not.
They are new<S>2319</S> every morning<S>1242</S>: great<S>7227</S> is thy faithfulness<S>530</S>.
The LORD<S>3068</S> is my portion<S>2506</S>, saith<S>559</S> my soul<S>5315</S>; therefore will I hope<S>3176</S> in him.
The LORD<S>3068</S> is good<S>2896</S> unto them that wait<S>6960</S> for him, to the soul<S>5315</S> that seeketh<S>1875</S> him.
It is good<S>2896</S> that a man should both hope<S>3175</S> <S>2342</S> and quietly wait<S>1748</S> for the salvation<S>8668</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>.
It is good<S>2896</S> for a man<S>1397</S> that he bear<S>5375</S> the yoke<S>5923</S> in his youth<S>5271</S>.
He sitteth<S>3427</S> alone<S>910</S> and keepeth silence<S>1826</S>, because he hath borne<S>5190</S> it upon him.
He putteth<S>5414</S> his mouth<S>6310</S> in the dust<S>6083</S>; if so be there may be<S>3426</S> hope<S>8615</S>.
He giveth<S>5414</S> his cheek<S>3895</S> to him that smiteth<S>5221</S> him: he is filled full<S>7646</S> with reproach<S>2781</S>.
For the Lord<S>136</S> will not cast off<S>2186</S> for ever<S>5769</S>:
But though he cause grief<S>3013</S>, yet will he have compassion<S>7355</S> according to the multitude<S>7230</S> of his mercies<S>2617</S>.
For he doth not afflict<S>6031</S> willingly<S>3820</S> nor grieve<S>3013</S> the children<S>1121</S> of men<S>376</S>. <sup>willingly: Heb. from his heart</sup>
To crush<S>1792</S> under his feet<S>7272</S> all the prisoners<S>615</S> of the earth<S>776</S>,
To turn aside<S>5186</S> the right<S>4941</S> of a man<S>1397</S> before the face<S>6440</S> of the most High<S>5945</S>, <sup>the most High: or, a superior</sup>
To subvert<S>5791</S> a man<S>120</S> in his cause<S>7379</S>, the Lord<S>136</S> approveth<S>7200</S> not. <sup>approveth not: or, seeth not</sup>
Who is he that saith<S>559</S>, and it cometh to pass, when the Lord<S>136</S> commandeth<S>6680</S> it not?
Out of the mouth<S>6310</S> of the most High<S>5945</S> proceedeth<S>3318</S> not evil<S>7451</S> and good<S>2896</S>?
Wherefore doth a living<S>2416</S> man<S>120</S> complain<S>596</S>, a man<S>1397</S> for the punishment of his sins<S>2399</S>? <sup>complain: or, murmur</sup>
Let us search<S>2664</S> and try<S>2713</S> our ways<S>1870</S>, and turn again<S>7725</S> to the LORD<S>3068</S>.
Let us lift up<S>5375</S> our heart<S>3824</S> with our hands<S>3709</S> unto God<S>410</S> in the heavens<S>8064</S>.
We<S>5168</S> have transgressed<S>6586</S> and have rebelled<S>4784</S>: thou hast not pardoned<S>5545</S>.
Thou hast covered<S>5526</S> with anger<S>639</S>, and persecuted<S>7291</S> us: thou hast slain<S>2026</S>, thou hast not pitied<S>2550</S>.
Thou hast covered<S>5526</S> thyself with a cloud<S>6051</S>, that our prayer<S>8605</S> should not pass through<S>5674</S>.
Thou hast made<S>7760</S> us as the offscouring<S>5501</S> and refuse<S>3973</S> in the midst<S>7130</S> of the people<S>5971</S>.
All our enemies<S>341</S> have opened<S>6475</S> their mouths<S>6310</S> against us.
Fear<S>6343</S> and a snare<S>6354</S> is come upon us, desolation<S>7612</S> and destruction<S>7667</S>.
Mine eye<S>5869</S> runneth down<S>3381</S> with rivers<S>6388</S> of water<S>4325</S> for the destruction<S>7667</S> of the daughter<S>1323</S> of my people<S>5971</S>.
Mine eye<S>5869</S> trickleth down<S>5064</S>, and ceaseth<S>1820</S> not, without any intermission<S>2014</S>,
Till the LORD<S>3068</S> look down<S>8259</S>, and behold<S>7200</S> from heaven<S>8064</S>.
Mine eye<S>5869</S> affecteth<S>5953</S> mine heart<S>5315</S> because of all the daughters<S>1323</S> of my city<S>5892</S>. <sup>mine heart: Heb. my soul</sup> <sup>because: or, more than all</sup>
Mine enemies<S>341</S> chased<S>6679</S> me sore<S>6679</S>, like a bird<S>6833</S>, without cause<S>2600</S>.
They have cut off<S>6789</S> my life<S>2416</S> in the dungeon<S>953</S>, and cast<S>3034</S> a stone<S>68</S> upon me.
Waters<S>4325</S> flowed over<S>6687</S> mine head<S>7218</S>; then I said<S>559</S>, I am cut off<S>1504</S>.
I called<S>7121</S> upon thy name<S>8034</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, out of the low<S>8482</S> dungeon<S>953</S>.
Thou hast heard<S>8085</S> my voice<S>6963</S>: hide<S>5956</S> not thine ear<S>241</S> at my breathing<S>7309</S>, at my cry<S>7775</S>.
Thou drewest near<S>7126</S> in the day<S>3117</S> that I called<S>7121</S> upon thee: thou saidst<S>559</S>, Fear<S>3372</S> not.
O Lord<S>136</S>, thou hast pleaded<S>7378</S> the causes<S>7379</S> of my soul<S>5315</S>; thou hast redeemed<S>1350</S> my life<S>2416</S>.
O LORD<S>3068</S>, thou hast seen<S>7200</S> my wrong<S>5792</S>: judge<S>8199</S> thou my cause<S>4941</S>.
Thou hast seen<S>7200</S> all their vengeance<S>5360</S> and all their imaginations<S>4284</S> against me.
Thou hast heard<S>8085</S> their reproach<S>2781</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, and all their imaginations<S>4284</S> against me;
The lips<S>8193</S> of those that rose up<S>6965</S> against me, and their device<S>1902</S> against me all the day<S>3117</S>.
Behold<S>5027</S> their sitting down<S>3427</S>, and their rising up<S>7012</S>; I am their musick<S>4485</S>.
Render<S>7725</S> unto them a recompence<S>1576</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, according to the work<S>4639</S> of their hands<S>3027</S>.
Give<S>5414</S> them sorrow<S>4044</S> of heart<S>3820</S>, thy curse<S>8381</S> unto them. <sup>sorrow: or, obstinacy of heart</sup>
Persecute<S>7291</S> and destroy<S>8045</S> them in anger<S>639</S> from under the heavens<S>8064</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>.

4.text: How is the gold<S>2091</S> become dim<S>6004</S>! how is the most<S>2896</S> fine gold<S>3800</S> changed<S>8132</S>! the stones<S>68</S> of the sanctuary<S>6944</S> are poured out<S>8210</S> in the top<S>7218</S> of every street<S>2351</S>.

The precious<S>3368</S> sons<S>1121</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>, comparable<S>5537</S> to fine gold<S>6337</S>, how are they esteemed<S>2803</S> as earthen<S>2789</S> pitchers<S>5035</S>, the work<S>4639</S> of the hands<S>3027</S> of the potter<S>3335</S>!

Even the sea monsters<S>8577</S> draw out<S>2502</S> the breast<S>7699</S>, they give suck<S>3243</S> to their young ones<S>1482</S>: the daughter<S>1323</S> of my people<S>5971</S> is become cruel<S>393</S>, like the ostriches<S>3283</S> in the wilderness<S>4057</S>. <sup>sea: or, sea calves</sup>

The tongue<S>3956</S> of the sucking child<S>3243</S> cleaveth<S>1692</S> to the roof of his mouth<S>2441</S> for thirst<S>6772</S>: the young children<S>5768</S> ask<S>7592</S> bread<S>3899</S>, and no man breaketh<S>6566</S> it unto them.
They that did feed<S>398</S> delicately<S>4574</S> are desolate<S>8074</S> in the streets<S>2351</S>: they that were brought up<S>539</S> in scarlet<S>8438</S> embrace<S>2263</S> dunghills<S>830</S>.

For the punishment of the iniquity<S>5771</S> of the daughter<S>1323</S> of my people<S>5971</S> is greater<S>1431</S> than the punishment<S>2403</S> of the sin of Sodom<S>5467</S>, that was overthrown<S>2015</S> as in a moment<S>7281</S>, and no hands<S>3027</S> stayed<S>2342</S> on her. <sup>punishment of the iniquity: or, iniquity</sup>

Her Nazarites<S>5139</S> were purer<S>2141</S> than snow<S>7950</S>, they were whiter<S>6705</S> than milk<S>2461</S>, they were more ruddy<S>119</S> in body<S>6106</S> than rubies<S>6443</S>, their polishing<S>1508</S> was of sapphire<S>5601</S>:

Their visage<S>8389</S> is blacker<S>2821</S> than a coal<S>7815</S>; they are not known<S>5234</S> in the streets<S>2351</S>: their skin<S>5785</S> cleaveth<S>6821</S> to their bones<S>6106</S>; it is withered<S>3001</S>, it is become like a stick<S>6086</S>. <sup>blacker: Heb. darker than blackness</sup>

They that be slain<S>2491</S> with the sword<S>2719</S> are better<S>2896</S> than they that be slain<S>2491</S> with hunger<S>7458</S>: for these<S>1992</S> pine away<S>2100</S>, stricken<S>1856</S> through for want of the fruits<S>8570</S> of the field<S>7704</S>. <sup>pine: Heb. flow out</sup>

The hands<S>3027</S> of the pitiful<S>7362</S> women<S>802</S> have sodden<S>1310</S> their own children<S>3206</S>: they were their meat<S>1262</S> in the destruction<S>7667</S> of the daughter<S>1323</S> of my people<S>5971</S>.

The LORD<S>3068</S> hath accomplished<S>3615</S> his fury<S>2534</S>; he hath poured out<S>8210</S> his fierce<S>2740</S> anger<S>639</S>, and hath kindled<S>3341</S> a fire<S>784</S> in Zion<S>6726</S>, and it hath devoured<S>398</S> the foundations<S>3247</S> thereof.

The kings<S>4428</S> of the earth<S>776</S>, and all the inhabitants<S>3427</S> of the world<S>8398</S>, would not have believed<S>539</S> that the adversary<S>6862</S> and the enemy<S>341</S> should have entered<S>935</S> into the gates<S>8179</S> of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>.

For the sins<S>2403</S> of her prophets<S>5030</S>, and the iniquities<S>5771</S> of her priests<S>3548</S>, that have shed<S>8210</S> the blood<S>1818</S> of the just<S>6662</S> in the midst<S>7130</S> of her,

They have wandered<S>5128</S> as blind<S>5787</S> men in the streets<S>2351</S>, they have polluted<S>1351</S> themselves with blood<S>1818</S>, so that men could<S>3201</S> not<S>3808</S> touch<S>5060</S> their garments<S>3830</S>. <sup>so: or, in that they could not but touch</sup>

They cried<S>7121</S> unto them, Depart<S>5493</S> ye; it is unclean<S>2931</S>; depart<S>5493</S>, depart<S>5493</S>, touch<S>5060</S> not: when they fled away<S>5132</S> and wandered<S>5128</S>, they said<S>559</S> among the heathen<S>1471</S>, They shall no more<S>3254</S> sojourn<S>1481</S> there. <sup>it: or, ye polluted</sup>

The anger<S>6440</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> hath divided<S>2505</S> them; he will no more<S>3254</S> regard<S>5027</S> them: they respected<S>5375</S> not the persons<S>6440</S> of the priests<S>3548</S>, they favoured<S>2603</S> not the elders<S>2205</S>. <sup>anger: or, face</sup>

As for us, our eyes<S>5869</S> as yet failed<S>3615</S> for our vain<S>1892</S> help<S>5833</S>: in our watching<S>6836</S> we have watched<S>6822</S> for a nation<S>1471</S> that could not save<S>3467</S> us.

They hunt<S>6679</S> our steps<S>6806</S>, that we cannot go<S>3212</S> in our streets<S>7339</S>: our end<S>7093</S> is near<S>7126</S>, our days<S>3117</S> are fulfilled<S>4390</S>; for our end<S>7093</S> is come<S>935</S>.

Our persecutors<S>7291</S> are swifter<S>7031</S> than the eagles<S>5404</S> of the heaven<S>8064</S>: they pursued<S>1814</S> us upon the mountains<S>2022</S>, they laid wait<S>693</S> for us in the wilderness<S>4057</S>.

The breath<S>7307</S> of our nostrils<S>639</S>, the anointed<S>4899</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>, was taken<S>3920</S> in their pits<S>7825</S>, of whom we said<S>559</S>, Under his shadow<S>6738</S> we shall live<S>2421</S> among the heathen<S>1471</S>.

Rejoice<S>7797</S> and be glad<S>8055</S>, O daughter<S>1323</S> of Edom<S>123</S>, that dwellest<S>3427</S> in the land<S>776</S> of Uz<S>5780</S>; the cup<S>3563</S> also shall pass<S>5674</S> through unto thee: thou shalt be drunken<S>7937</S>, and shalt make thyself naked<S>6168</S>.

The punishment of thine iniquity<S>5771</S> is accomplished<S>8552</S>, O daughter<S>1323</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>; he will no more<S>3254</S> carry thee away into captivity<S>1540</S>: he will visit<S>6485</S> thine iniquity<S>5771</S>, O daughter<S>1323</S> of Edom<S>123</S>; he will discover<S>1540</S> thy sins<S>2403</S>. <sup>The: or, Thine iniquity</sup> <sup>discover: or, carry thee captive for thy sins</sup>

5.text: Remember<S>2142</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, what is come upon us: consider<S>5027</S>, and behold<S>7200</S> our reproach<S>2781</S>.
Our inheritance<S>5159</S> is turned<S>2015</S> to strangers<S>2114</S>, our houses<S>1004</S> to aliens<S>5237</S>.
We are orphans<S>3490</S> and fatherless<S>369</S> <S>1</S>, our mothers<S>517</S> are as widows<S>490</S>.
We have drunken<S>8354</S> our water<S>4325</S> for money<S>3701</S>; our wood<S>6086</S> is sold<S>935</S> <S>4242</S> unto us. <sup>is: Heb. cometh for price</sup>
Our necks<S>6677</S> are under persecution<S>7291</S>: we labour<S>3021</S>, and have no rest<S>5117</S>. <sup>Our: Heb. On our necks are we persecuted</sup>
We have given<S>5414</S> the hand<S>3027</S> to the Egyptians<S>4714</S>, and to the Assyrians<S>804</S>, to be satisfied<S>7646</S> with bread<S>3899</S>.
Our fathers<S>1</S> have sinned<S>2398</S>, and are not<S>369</S>; and we have borne<S>5445</S> their iniquities<S>5771</S>.
Servants<S>5650</S> have ruled<S>4910</S> over us: there is none that doth deliver<S>6561</S> us out of their hand<S>3027</S>.
We gat<S>935</S> our bread<S>3899</S> with the peril of our lives<S>5315</S> because<S>6440</S> of the sword<S>2719</S> of the wilderness<S>4057</S>.
Our skin<S>5785</S> was black<S>3648</S> like an oven<S>8574</S> because<S>6440</S> of the terrible<S>2152</S> famine<S>7458</S>. <sup>terrible: or, terrors, or, storms</sup>
They ravished<S>6031</S> the women<S>802</S> in Zion<S>6726</S>, and the maids<S>1330</S> in the cities<S>5892</S> of Judah<S>3063</S>.
Princes<S>8269</S> are hanged up<S>8518</S> by their hand<S>3027</S>: the faces<S>6440</S> of elders<S>2205</S> were not honoured<S>1921</S>.
They took<S>5375</S> the young men<S>970</S> to grind<S>2911</S>, and the children<S>5288</S> fell<S>3782</S> under the wood<S>6086</S>.
The elders<S>2205</S> have ceased<S>7673</S> from the gate<S>8179</S>, the young men<S>970</S> from their musick<S>5058</S>.
The joy<S>4885</S> of our heart<S>3820</S> is ceased<S>7673</S>; our dance<S>4234</S> is turned<S>2015</S> into mourning<S>60</S>.
The crown<S>5850</S> is fallen<S>5307</S> from our head<S>7218</S>: woe<S>188</S> unto us, that we have sinned<S>2398</S>! <sup>The: Heb. The crown of our head is fallen</sup>
For this our heart<S>3820</S> is faint<S>1739</S>; for these things our eyes<S>5869</S> are dim<S>2821</S>.
Because of the mountain<S>2022</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>, which is desolate<S>8074</S>, the foxes<S>7776</S> walk<S>1980</S> upon it.
Thou, O LORD<S>3068</S>, remainest<S>3427</S> for ever<S>5769</S>; thy throne<S>3678</S> from generation<S>1755</S> to generation<S>1755</S>.
Wherefore dost thou forget<S>7911</S> us for ever<S>5331</S>, and forsake<S>5800</S> us so long<S>753</S> time<S>3117</S>? <sup>so: Heb. for length of days?</sup>
Turn<S>7725</S> thou us unto thee, O LORD<S>3068</S>, and we shall be turned<S>7725</S>; renew<S>2318</S> our days<S>3117</S> as of old<S>6924</S>.
But thou hast utterly<S>3988</S> rejected<S>3988</S> us; thou art very<S>3966</S> wroth<S>7107</S> against us. <sup>But: or, For wilt thou utterly reject us?</sup>
